query: My fraud classifier has 99% accuracy on the test set. Sounds great, right?
expected_behavior: Flags class imbalance (fraud is typically rare, so 99% accuracy is suspicious — likely the always-predict-not-fraud baseline) as the dominant explanation
Requests the class distribution (positive rate / minority-class fraction) before accepting the accuracy number
Recommends PR-AUC and per-class recall (or MCC) over bare accuracy as the right framing for an imbalanced classifier
